---
title: 'Job Mentioned in other books'
date: '2013-10-16T17:07:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '4723189674797480206'
blogger_filename: '/2013/10/job-mentioned-in-other-books.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Job Research'
  - 'Job'
---

Job Mentioned outside of the book of Job<br /><br /><blockquote class="tr_bq"><h4 class="ResultTitle"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/kjv/james/5-11.html">James 5:11 KJV</a></h4><div class="ResultText"><i>Behold  , we count them happy   which endure  . Ye have heard   of the <span class="Highlight">patience</span>  of Job,  and  have seen   the end  of the Lord;  that  the Lord  is   very pitiful,  and  of tender mercy.&nbsp;</i></div><div class="ResultText"></div><div class="ResultText"><span class="versetext" id="jas5-7" style="display: inline;"><span class="strongs"></span>   </span>          <span class="versetext" id="jas5-8" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">8</span>    <span class="strongs">Be</span>  <span class="strongs">ye</span> <span class="strongs">also</span> <span class="strongs">patient</span> ; <span class="strongs">establish</span>  <span class="strongs">your</span> <span class="strongs">hearts:</span> <span class="strongs">for</span> the <span class="strongs">coming</span> of the <span class="strongs">Lord</span> draweth <span class="strongs">nigh</span> .    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="jas5-9" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">9</span>    <span class="strongs">Grudge</span>  <span class="strongs">not</span><a href="http://draft.blogger.com/null" name="a"></a> one against <span class="strongs">another</span> <span class="strongs"></span>, <span class="strongs">brethren,</span> <span class="strongs">lest</span> ye be <span class="strongs">condemned</span> : <span class="strongs">behold</span> , the <span class="strongs">judge</span> <span class="strongs">standeth</span>   <span class="strongs">before</span> the <span class="strongs">door.</span>   </span>          <span class="versetext" id="jas5-10" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">10</span>    <span class="strongs">Take</span> , <span class="strongs">my</span> <span class="strongs">brethren,</span> the <span class="strongs">prophets,</span> <span class="strongs">who</span> have <span class="strongs">spoken</span>  in the <span class="strongs">name</span> of the <span class="strongs">Lord,</span> for an <span class="strongs">example</span> of suffering <span class="strongs">affliction,</span> <span class="strongs">and</span> of <span class="strongs">patience.</span>   </span>          <span class="versetext" id="jas5-11" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">11</span>    <span class="strongs">Behold</span> , we count them <span class="strongs">happy</span>  which <span class="strongs">endure</span> . Ye have <span class="strongs">heard</span>  of the <span class="strongs">patience</span> of <span class="strongs">Job,</span> <span class="strongs">and</span> have <span class="strongs">seen</span>  the <span class="strongs">end</span> of the <span class="strongs">Lord;</span> <span class="strongs">that</span> the <span class="strongs">Lord</span> <span class="strongs">is</span>  very <span class="strongs">pitiful,</span> <span class="strongs">and</span> of tender <span class="strongs">mercy.</span>   </span>          <span class="versetext" id="jas5-12" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">12</span>    <span class="strongs">But</span> <span class="strongs">above</span> all <span class="strongs">things,</span> <span class="strongs">my</span> <span class="strongs">brethren,</span> <span class="strongs">swear</span>  <span class="strongs">not,</span> <span class="strongs">neither</span> by <span class="strongs">heaven,</span> <span class="strongs">neither</span> by the <span class="strongs">earth,</span> <span class="strongs">neither</span> by <span class="strongs">any</span> <span class="strongs">other</span> <span class="strongs">oath:</span> <span class="strongs">but</span> <span class="strongs">let</span>  <span class="strongs">your</span> <span class="strongs">yea</span> <span class="strongs">be</span>  <span class="strongs">yea;</span> <span class="strongs">and</span> your <span class="strongs">nay,</span> <span class="strongs">nay;</span> <span class="strongs">lest</span> ye <span class="strongs">fall</span>  <span class="strongs">into</span> <span class="strongs">condemnation.</span>   </span>          <span class="versetext" id="jas5-13" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">13</span>    <span class="strongs">Is</span>  <span class="strongs">any</span> <span class="strongs">among</span> <span class="strongs">you</span> <span class="strongs">afflicted</span> ? let him <span class="strongs">pray</span> . <span class="strongs">Is</span>  <span class="strongs">any</span> <span class="strongs">merry</span> ? let him sing <span class="strongs">psalms</span> .    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="jas5-14" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">14</span>    <span class="strongs">Is</span>  <span class="strongs">any</span> <span class="strongs">sick</span>  <span class="strongs">among</span> <span class="strongs">you?</span> let him <span class="strongs">call</span>  for the <span class="strongs">elders</span> of the <span class="strongs">church;</span> <span class="strongs">and</span> let them <span class="strongs">pray</span>  <span class="strongs">over</span> <span class="strongs">him,</span> <span class="strongs">anointing</span>  <span class="strongs">him</span> with <span class="strongs">oil</span> <span class="strongs">in</span> the <span class="strongs">name</span> of the <span class="strongs">Lord:</span>   </span>          <span class="versetext" id="jas5-15" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">15</span>    <span class="strongs">And</span> the <span class="strongs">prayer</span> of <span class="strongs">faith</span> shall <span class="strongs">save</span>  the <span class="strongs">sick</span> , <span class="strongs">and</span> the <span class="strongs">Lord</span> shall <span class="strongs">raise</span>  <span class="strongs">him</span> <span class="strongs">up</span> ; and <span class="strongs">if</span> he <span class="strongs">have</span>  <span class="strongs">committed</span>  <span class="strongs">sins,</span> they shall be <span class="strongs">forgiven</span>  <span class="strongs">him.</span>   </span>          <span class="versetext" id="jas5-16" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">16</span>    <span class="strongs">Confess</span>  your <span class="strongs">faults</span> one to <span class="strongs">another,</span> <span class="strongs">and</span> <span class="strongs">pray</span>  <span class="strongs">one</span>  <span class="strongs">for</span> <span class="strongs">another,</span> <span class="strongs">that</span> ye may be <span class="strongs">healed</span> . The effectual <span class="strongs">fervent</span>  <span class="strongs">prayer</span> of a righteous <span class="strongs">man</span> <span class="strongs">availeth</span>  <span class="strongs">much.</span>&nbsp; </span></div></blockquote><div class="ResultText"><h4 class="ResultTitle"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/kjv/genesis/46-13.html">Genesis 46:13 KJV</a></h4><div class="ResultText">And the sons  of Issachar;  Tola,  and Phuvah,  and <span class="Highlight">Job</span>,  and Shimron.&nbsp;</div><div class="ResultText"></div><div class="ResultText"><blockquote class="tr_bq"><h4 class="ResultTitle"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/kjv/ezekiel/14-14.html">Ezekiel 14:14 KJV</a></h4><div class="ResultText">Though these three  men,  Noah,  Daniel,  and <span class="Highlight">Job</span>,  were in it,  they should deliver   but their own souls  by their righteousness,  saith   the Lord  GOD.&nbsp;</div><div class="ResultText"></div></blockquote><div class="ResultText"><blockquote><h4 class="ResultTitle"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/kjv/ezekiel/14-20.html">Ezekiel 14:20 KJV</a></h4><div class="ResultText">Though Noah,  Daniel,  and <span class="Highlight">Job</span>,   were in it,  as I live,  saith   the Lord  GOD,  they shall deliver    neither son  nor  daughter;  they shall but deliver   their own souls   by their righteousness.&nbsp;</div></blockquote><blockquote class="tr_bq"><span class="versetext" id="eze14-8" style="display: inline;">   </span>          <span class="versetext" id="eze14-9" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">"9</span>    And if the <span class="strongs">prophet</span> be <span class="strongs">deceived</span>  when he hath <span class="strongs">spoken</span>  a <span class="strongs">thing,</span> I the <span class="strongs">LORD</span> have <span class="strongs">deceived</span>  that <span class="strongs">prophet,</span> and I will stretch <span class="strongs">out</span>  my <span class="strongs">hand</span> upon him, and will <span class="strongs">destroy</span>  him from the <span class="strongs">midst</span> of my <span class="strongs">people</span> <span class="strongs">Israel.</span>   </span>          <span class="versetext" id="eze14-10" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">10</span>    And they shall <span class="strongs">bear</span>  the punishment of their <span class="strongs">iniquity:</span> the <span class="strongs">punishment</span> of the <span class="strongs">prophet</span> shall be even as the <span class="strongs">punishment</span> of him that <span class="strongs">seeketh</span>  unto him;    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="eze14-11" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">11</span>    That the <span class="strongs">house</span> of <span class="strongs">Israel</span> may go no more <span class="strongs">astray</span>  from <span class="strongs">me,</span> neither be <span class="strongs">polluted</span>  any more with all their <span class="strongs">transgressions;</span> but that they may be my <span class="strongs">people,</span> and I may be their <span class="strongs">God,</span> <span class="strongs">saith</span>  the <span class="strongs">Lord</span> <span class="strongs">GOD.</span>   </span>          <span class="versetext" id="eze14-12" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">12</span>    The <span class="strongs">word</span> of the <span class="strongs">LORD</span> came again to me, <span class="strongs">saying</span> ,    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="eze14-13" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">13</span>    <span class="strongs">Son</span> of <span class="strongs">man,</span> when the <span class="strongs">land</span> <span class="strongs">sinneth</span>  against me by <span class="strongs">trespassing</span>  <span class="strongs">grievously,</span> then will I stretch <span class="strongs">out</span>  mine <span class="strongs">hand</span> upon it, and will <span class="strongs">break</span>  the <span class="strongs">staff</span> of the <span class="strongs">bread</span> thereof, and will <span class="strongs">send</span>  <span class="strongs">famine</span> upon it, and will cut <span class="strongs">off</span>  <span class="strongs">man</span> and <span class="strongs">beast</span> from it:    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="eze14-14" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">14</span>    Though these <span class="strongs">three</span> <span class="strongs">men,</span> <u><span class="strongs">Noah,</span> <span class="strongs">Daniel,</span> and </u><span class="strongs"><u><b>Job</b></u>,</span> were in <span class="strongs">it,</span> they should <span class="strongs">deliver</span>  but their own <span class="strongs">souls</span> by their <span class="strongs">righteousness,</span> <span class="strongs">saith</span>  the <span class="strongs">Lord</span> <span class="strongs">GOD.</span>   </span>          <span class="versetext" id="eze14-15" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">15</span>    <span class="strongs">If</span> I cause <span class="strongs">noisome</span> <span class="strongs">beasts</span> to pass <span class="strongs">through</span>  the <span class="strongs">land,</span> and they <span class="strongs">spoil</span><a href="http://draft.blogger.com/null" name="a"></a>  it, so that it be <span class="strongs">desolate,</span> that no man may pass <span class="strongs">through</span>  <span class="strongs">because</span> of the <span class="strongs">beasts:</span>   </span>          <span class="versetext" id="eze14-16" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">16</span>    Though these <span class="strongs">three</span> <span class="strongs">men</span> were in <span class="strongs">it,</span><a href="http://draft.blogger.com/null" name="b"></a> as I <span class="strongs">live,</span> <span class="strongs">saith</span>  the <span class="strongs">Lord</span> <span class="strongs">GOD,</span> they shall <span class="strongs">deliver</span>  neither <span class="strongs">sons</span> nor <span class="strongs">daughters;</span> they only shall be <span class="strongs">delivered</span> , but the <span class="strongs">land</span> shall be <span class="strongs">desolate.</span>   </span>          <span class="versetext" id="eze14-17" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">17</span>    Or if I <span class="strongs">bring</span>  a <span class="strongs">sword</span> upon that <span class="strongs">land,</span> and <span class="strongs">say</span> , <span class="strongs">Sword,</span> go <span class="strongs">through</span>  the <span class="strongs">land;</span> so that I cut <span class="strongs">off</span>  <span class="strongs">man</span> and <span class="strongs">beast</span> from it:    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="eze14-18" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">18</span>    Though these <span class="strongs">three</span> <span class="strongs">men</span> were in <span class="strongs">it,</span> as I <span class="strongs">live,</span> <span class="strongs">saith</span>  the <span class="strongs">Lord</span> <span class="strongs">GOD,</span> they shall <span class="strongs">deliver</span>  neither <span class="strongs">sons</span> nor <span class="strongs">daughters,</span> but they only shall be <span class="strongs">delivered</span>  themselves.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="eze14-19" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">19</span>    Or if I <span class="strongs">send</span>  a <span class="strongs">pestilence</span> into that <span class="strongs">land,</span> and pour <span class="strongs">out</span>  my <span class="strongs">fury</span> upon it in <span class="strongs">blood,</span> to cut <span class="strongs">off</span>  from it <span class="strongs">man</span> and <span class="strongs">beast:</span>   </span>          <span class="versetext" id="eze14-20" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">20</span>    Though <u><span class="strongs">Noah,</span> <span class="strongs">Daniel,</span> and <span class="strongs"><b>Job</b>,</span></u> were in <span class="strongs">it,</span> as I <span class="strongs">live,</span> <span class="strongs">saith</span>  the <span class="strongs">Lord</span> <span class="strongs">GOD,</span> they shall <span class="strongs">deliver</span>  neither <span class="strongs">son</span> <span class="strongs">nor</span> <span class="strongs">daughter;</span> they shall but <span class="strongs">deliver</span>  their own <span class="strongs">souls</span> by their <span class="strongs">righteousness.</span>   </span>          <span class="versetext" id="eze14-21" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">21</span>    For thus <span class="strongs">saith</span>  the <span class="strongs">Lord</span> <span class="strongs">GOD;</span> How much more when I <span class="strongs">send</span>  my <span class="strongs">four</span> <span class="strongs">sore</span> <span class="strongs">judgments</span> upon <span class="strongs">Jerusalem,</span> the <span class="strongs">sword,</span> and the <span class="strongs">famine,</span> and the <span class="strongs">noisome</span> <span class="strongs">beast,</span> and the <span class="strongs">pestilence,</span> to cut <span class="strongs">off</span>  from it <span class="strongs">man</span> and <span class="strongs">beast?"</span></span></blockquote></div><div class="ResultText"></div><div class="ResultText"><span class="versetext" id="eze14-21" style="display: inline;"><span class="strongs">&nbsp;Three Righteous men... what do they have in common, or how are they related?</span></span></div><ul><li><span class="versetext" id="eze14-21" style="display: inline;"><span class="strongs">Noah</span></span></li><ul><li><span class="versetext" id="eze14-21" style="display: inline;"><span class="strongs">&nbsp;The last man of the old world, last man standing. Believed God and built an Ark to save mankind and all breathing animals. Survived the attack of the wrath of God against the sin of the world.</span></span></li></ul><li><span class="versetext" id="eze14-21" style="display: inline;"><span class="strongs">Daniel</span></span></li><ul><li><span class="versetext" id="eze14-21" style="display: inline;"><span class="strongs">&nbsp;Man who sought God in his career as a political and spiritual leader. Survived attack after attack after attack. Of the enemy.</span></span></li></ul><li><span class="versetext" id="eze14-21" style="display: inline;"><span class="strongs">Job&nbsp;</span></span></li><ul><li><span class="versetext" id="eze14-21" style="display: inline;"><span class="strongs">A man who was attacked because of his righteousness, but also because he allowed fear into his life.</span></span><span class="versetext" id="eze14-21" style="display: inline;"><span class="strongs"> </span></span></li></ul></ul></div></div><br /><br /><br /><blockquote class="tr_bq"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/kjv/proverbs/16-18.html" target="_blank">Proverbs 16:18 (King James Version)</a><br />18 Pride goeth before destruction, and an haughty spirit before a fall.  </blockquote><a href="http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JtmsLJWNfe8" target="_blank">See Satan and God, Pastor Morris</a><br /><br /><iframe allowfullscreen="" frameborder="0" height="315" src="//www.youtube.com/embed/JtmsLJWNfe8" width="420"></iframe>
